mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or positive returns exceed 55% when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an imminent labor market contraction that reduces consumer spending and corporate profit growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE < 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: High real savings funds combined with low federal funds rates create abundant cheap capital that flows into equities, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon when both conditions align.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline by >10% in out-of-sample Sharpe ratio or produces negative returns when RSAFS > 350k and FEDFUNDS < 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: An expanding industrial production index reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which boosts short-term corporate earnings momentum and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during inflationary supply bottlenecks or yields a negative 5-day return in >50% of expansionary observations.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates robust labor demand and rising household income, which supports consumer expenditure and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between PAYEMS growth and 20-day returns falls below 0.05 over any 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp year-over-year drop in housing starts signals a collapsing construction sector and weakening consumer confidence, transmitting negative sentiment to equity markets and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in >65% of triggered months or the threshold fails to separate bull/bear outcomes with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high inflation and low unemployment create a stagflationary risk premium that forces central banks to tighten policy aggressively, compressing equity valuations and yielding negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces positive returns in >55% of observations or fails to beat a simple CPIAUCSL-only model in out-of-sample accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP confirms broad-based economic expansion that lifts corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon when the economy is in a confirmed expansion phase.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power during recessionary quarters or produces negative 60-day returns in >50% of expansionary observations.
